title: podman play kube support for configMap volumeSource
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

Hello,

With podman v2.2.1, we are able to do  `podman play kube ... --configmap` and add configmaps. The current supported usage of config-maps in YAML is with `spec.containers[].envFrom.configMapRef`.

Is there a plan to support `spec.volumes.configMap` with `podman play kube`?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create pod.yaml with `spec.volumes.configMap` and a configmap.yaml wit a valid configmap

2. podman play kube pod.yaml --configmap configmap.yaml


**Describe the results you received:**
I believe you get something like: `HostPath is currently the only supported VolumeSource`

**Describe the results you expected:**
Support for `spec.volumes.configMap`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Dec 17 11:10:45 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.18.0
  cgroupManager: systemd

  conmon:

    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.21, commit: 4cff974a37907e701aa2e31ad480ccc6f3f586f5'
  cpus: 10
  distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "8.2"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: rhel82
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 4.18.0-193.el8.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic

  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-65.rc10.module+el8.2.0+5762+aaee29fb.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  os: linux

  swapFree: 4001624064

    number: 4

    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage

    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  imageStore:
    number: 16
  runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
N/A

comments.body: Interested in opening a PR to add this functionality?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I'm working on this.
Any news on that? Especially asking @xordspar0.
@xordspar0: I've seen you created the branch https://github.com/xordspar0/podman/tree/configMapVolumes, but you didn't push any commits yet, right?

Thanks for your interest. I am still working on it in my free time. I have a pile of unfinished changes on my laptop. I may as well commit and push them. I'm not sure if I'm going in the right direction yet. The main thing I haven't figured out yet is where to store the data that will be mounted as a volume. If anyone has any ideas about that or areas of the codebase I can investigate to learn more, please let me know.

There, it's rebased and pushed: https://github.com/xordspar0/podman/tree/configMapVolumes

Thank you @xordspar0. In your comment you say it's not working yet. I looked at the commit and can imagine what you do, but I am far away from understanding and being able to help or finish that work. 
Will you have time to finish that work or maybe someone else can assist/finish that work?

One question:
In pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go line 228-238 you implement registryCreds an dockerRegistryOptions. This has nothing to do with configMapVolumes, has it? :o)
Oops, thanks for catching that. That must have been a mistake I made when I rebased on master. I'll delete that.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@xordspar0 Any progress on this?
No; I had very limited Internet access in the last few weeks. Unfortunately the tests require an Internet connection to work. I'm re-connected now though. I'm planning to resume work tomorrow.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@xordspar0 Any progress?
Yes, I have been making slow progress in [my branch](https://github.com/xordspar0/podman/tree/configMapVolumes), but it's not done yet.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@xordspar0 any update?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@rhatdan please remove the stale status. :)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@rhatdan please remove the stale status. :)
@umohnani8 PTAL
@xordspar0 are you still working on this? If not, I can take over

I got stuck a while back. Feel free to take a look at my branch and do something different. :slightly_smiling_face: I'll keep checking in here as well, so I can take over eventually if it doesn't get done.
Woo, nice work @umohnani8! :tada: I look forward to seeing this in the next release!
